title: Expressing Opinion
dialogue: A: What do you think about online learning?
B: In my opinion, it is useful, but students still need classroom interaction.

practice.answerKey: In my opinion, volunteering is important because it teaches responsibility and solidarity.

title: Agreeing and Disagreeing
dialogue: A: Students should use phones only for learning.
B: I completely agree. Phones can distract students if they are not used wisely.

practice.task: Disagree politely with this idea: Exams are useless.
practice.answerKey: I am afraid I disagree. Exams can help students organize their revision and measure progress.

title: Making Requests

formal: I would be grateful if you could ...

dialogue: A: Could you please explain the passive voice again?
B: Of course. Passive voice uses be plus past participle.

practice.task: Ask your teacher politely to correct your essay.
practice.answerKey: Could you please correct my essay when you have time?

title: Giving Advice
dialogue: A: I am worried about the English exam.
B: You should practice past papers and write one paragraph every day.

practice.task: Advise a friend who wastes time on social media.
practice.answerKey: You should make a revision timetable and turn off notifications while studying.

title: Complaining

formal: I am writing to complain about ...
I would like to draw your attention to ...

dialogue: A: I am writing to complain about the noise near the library.
B: Thank you for informing us. We will look into the problem.

practice.task: Complain politely about a broken classroom window.
practice.answerKey: I would like to draw your attention to a broken window in our classroom.

title: Apologizing

formal: I am sorry for the inconvenience.

dialogue: A: I am sorry for submitting the homework late.
B: It is all right, but please respect the deadline next time.

practice.task: Apologize for missing a group meeting.
practice.answerKey: I sincerely apologize for missing the group meeting. I had an urgent family matter.

title: Expressing Purpose
dialogue: A: Why did the students organize the campaign?
B: They organized it in order to raise awareness about recycling.

practice.task: Join the sentences: Students use dictionaries. They want to learn new words.
practice.answerKey: Students use dictionaries in order to learn new words.

title: Expressing Cause and Effect
dialogue: A: Why did many students join the club?
B: Because they wanted to improve their speaking skills.

practice.task: Show effect: The city planted trees. The air became cleaner.
practice.answerKey: The city planted trees; as a result, the air became cleaner.

title: Expressing Addition and Contrast
dialogue: A: Online learning is flexible.
B: Moreover, it gives students access to many resources. However, it needs self-discipline.

practice.task: Combine with contrast: Technology is useful. It can waste time.
practice.answerKey: Technology is useful; however, it can waste time.

title: Asking For and Giving Clarification

formal: Could you explain what you mean?

dialogue: A: This answer needs evidence.
B: Could you explain what you mean?
A: I mean you should quote a short phrase from the text.

practice.task: Ask for clarification when you do not understand a grammar rule.
practice.answerKey: Could you explain this grammar rule again, please?

title: Inviting

formal: We would be pleased to invite you to ...
informal: Do you want to come to ...?

dialogue: A: Would you like to attend our English club presentation?
B: Thank you. I would be happy to attend.

practice.task: Invite a friend to a school environmental campaign.
practice.answerKey: Would you like to join our school environmental campaign this Saturday?

title: Accepting and Refusing Invitations

formal: I would be delighted to attend.
I regret that I cannot attend.

dialogue: A: Would you like to join our study group?
B: That sounds great. What time shall we meet?

practice.task: Refuse an invitation politely because you have an exam.
practice.answerKey: Thank you for your invitation, but I regret that I cannot attend because I have an exam.

title: Making Suggestions

formal: It would be a good idea to ...

dialogue: A: Our class needs more speaking practice.
B: Why do not we create an English conversation club?

practice.task: Suggest a solution for plastic waste at school.
practice.answerKey: It would be a good idea to place recycling bins in the schoolyard.

title: Expressing Certainty and Uncertainty
dialogue: A: Do you think artificial intelligence will change education?
B: I am certain that it will help learning, but I am not sure it can replace teachers.

practice.answerKey: I am not sure whether studying abroad is the best choice for every student.
